main_turf.bias_notes: Clockwise. A sprawling, expansive galloping track and the premier venue in Queensland. The massive 2,027m circumference and sweeping turns allow large-striding horses to maintain their momentum. The defining feature is the 434m home straight, which has a subtle, exhausting uphill incline from the 800m mark to the winning post. This gradient heavily tests stamina, frequently allowing deep closers ('Swoopers') to run down tiring leaders in the final 100m.
main_turf.post_bias: In sprints (1000m to 1200m), middle-to-wide draws (5-8) often fare surprisingly well as riders have plenty of time to find cover and avoid early speed duels. In route and staying races (1600m+), inside barriers (1-4) hold a significant advantage by saving ground around the long, sweeping turns.

handicapping_angles.the_uphill_stamina_test: Heavily downgrade pure 'Need-the-Lead' sprinters, particularly those shipping in from tighter tracks like Doomben. The 434m uphill straight exposes horses lacking genuine stamina, making this one of the best tracks in Australia to back 'Swoopers'.
handicapping_angles.the_doomben_flip: Eagle Farm is directly across the road from Doomben, but they play completely differently. Doomben is a tight front-runner's track; Eagle Farm is a sprawling closer's track. Aggressively upgrade horses who ran well late at Doomben but ran out of room, as they will relish the massive open spaces of Eagle Farm.
handicapping_angles.big_striding_gallopers: Large, long-striding horses who often become unbalanced on tight provincial circuits (like Kilcoy or Ipswich) absolutely thrive here. Upgrade horses described in form guides as having a 'bounding' or long-reaching action.
